content_en: Hence, he decided to have him removed from Damascus. Al-Nawawi said that he would listen and obeyperhaps realizing that such would cause less harm and civil strife then remaining in Damascus and opposing the ruler. Hence, he left Damascus and went to his hometown ofNawa.4 1 Recorded by Muslim. 2 The complete wording of the letter may be found in ibn al-Attaar, pp. 108-1 14; al-Suyooti, al­ Minhaaj, pp. 71-74; al-Haddaad, pp. 1 1 1-1 13; Salmaan, al-Rudood, pp. 310-3 13; al-Diqr, pp. 146-1 49. 3 See al-Diqr, p. 144. 4 Al-Diqr, p. 145. The Life of Imam al-Nawawi Al-Nawawi, the Ruler and the Masses Once Again Sultan al-Dhaahir Baibars understood the dangers of the Mongols close by. Hence, he decided to do what he could to defeat them and drive them further out of the lands of the Muslims. He asked for afatwa from the scholars that would allow him to take money from the citizenry in order to use it in his battles against the Mongols. The scholars of al-Shaam wrote such a fatwa for him. He asked if any scholar refused to write such a letter and he was told that only al-Nawawi refused. Al-Dhaahir Baibars had al-Nawawi brought to him and he asked him personally to sign with the other scholars. However, al­ Nawawi refused. The Sultan asked him why. He answered, I know that you used to be a slave of the Ameer Banduqaar and you did not have any wealth. Then Allah bestowed His bounty upon you and you became the ruler. I have heard that you have one thousand male slaves and each one of them as a girth [by which the horse's saddle is tied] of gold. And you have two hundred slave girls and each of them has a portion of gold jewelry. If you spend all of that and you leave your slaves with straps and dyes instead of the gold girths, and you leave the slave girls with their clothing and no jewelry, then I will give you afatwa that you can take wealth from the citizenry.1 Here, once again, it is narrated that the Sultan got upset with him and had him banished from Damascus. He then left to Nawa. Perhaps, there was more than one event that led to the Sultan's actual decision to ban al-Nawawi from Damascus, Allah knows best. After he left to Nawa, the scholars said that he was one of their leading scholars and workers. He is someone whose example is to be followed. Hence, they went to try to bring him back to Damascus. However, he refused. He said that he would not enter Damascus if al-Dhaahir was still there. After a few months, Sultan al-Dhaahir Baibars died.2 Al-Jaami' commented on this incident by stating that one should note how the Sultan was not willing to take any action until he received the fatwa of the scholars on that matter. Even though the Sultan wanted that wealth for a noble purpose, al-Nawawi refused as he saw it as taking wealth through improper means.3 Pious intentions do not make illegal means permissible. Finally, al-Jaami stated that one should note how the rest of the scholars reacted. They did not remain silent at what had happened to their fellow scholar and leader. Instead, they got together and tried to bring al1 See Abdul Rahmaan al-Jaami, Ulama'una wa Duatuna: Mawaaqif Batoola wa Shujaah ిRiyadh: Daar al-Watn, 141 1 A.H,), p. 37. Ibid., p. 38. 3 Ibid., p. 38. Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi Nawawi back to Damascus, showing him that they would support him upon his return. This is the behavior that scholars should exhibit towards one another.1 As shall be seen in the next example, al-Nawawi had already set the example for them. He is the one who came to their defense when the ruler tried to wrong them. Al-Nawawi, the Ruler and the Scholars On one occasion, the ruler decreed that a jurist could only teach at one school at a time. This caused great hardship upon the scholars as many of the schools did not offer sufficient salaries for them to live. Hence, they had to teach in different schools to make ends meet. Al-Nawawi knew the importance of the scholars and knew the importance of standing up on their behalf. Hence, he did not remain silent on this issue but he once again wrote the ruler to correct him on this policy that he had enacted. This shows that al-Nawawi involved himself in social issues that were not directly related to texts of the Quran and sunnah. No one can claim that this decree was not a right of the ruler if he felt that there was some benefit or need for it. However, al-Nawawi realized that such a ruling was not in the best interests of the people or, in particular, of the scholars who are so important to society. Hence, he stepped in and did his best to change the way of the ruler.
content_id:
Akhirnya penguasa memutuskan untuk mengusir al-Nawawi dari Damaskus. Al-Nawawi menyatakan bahwa ia akan mendengar dan menaati — mungkin karena ia menyadari bahwa hal itu akan menimbulkan kerusakan dan fitnah yang lebih kecil daripada tetap tinggal di Damaskus dan terus berhadapan dengan penguasa. Maka ia meninggalkan Damaskus dan kembali ke kampung halamannya, Nawa.

Pada kesempatan lain, Sultan al-Zahir Baibars memahami bahaya yang ditimbulkan oleh keberadaan bangsa Mongol di dekat wilayah Muslim. Ia meminta fatwa dari para ulama yang membolehkannya memungut uang dari rakyat untuk membiayai peperangan melawan Mongol. Para ulama Syam mengeluarkan fatwa tersebut untuknya. Ketika ditanya apakah ada ulama yang menolak, ia diberitahu bahwa hanya al-Nawawi yang menolak. Al-Zahir Baibars pun memanggil al-Nawawi dan memintanya secara langsung untuk ikut menandatangani fatwa bersama ulama-ulama lainnya. Namun al-Nawawi tetap menolak. Sultan bertanya mengapa. Al-Nawawi menjawab: 'Aku tahu bahwa engkau dulu adalah budak Amir Banduqar dan tidak memiliki harta apa pun. Kemudian Allah menganugerahkan karunia-Nya kepadamu dan engkau menjadi penguasa. Aku mendengar bahwa engkau memiliki seribu budak laki-laki dan masing-masing memiliki sabuk emas, serta dua ratus budak perempuan dan masing-masing memiliki perhiasan emas. Jika engkau menghabiskan semua itu dan membiarkan budak-budakmu hanya dengan tali biasa, dan membiarkan budak-budak perempuanmu hanya dengan pakaian tanpa perhiasan, maka aku akan memberikan fatwa bahwa engkau boleh mengambil harta dari rakyat.'

Sultan pun marah dan mengusirnya dari Damaskus. Al-Nawawi pergi ke Nawa. Para ulama kemudian berkata bahwa ia adalah salah satu ulama dan pekerja terdepan mereka, seseorang yang layak diteladani. Maka mereka berusaha membawanya kembali ke Damaskus, namun ia menolak. Ia berkata tidak akan masuk Damaskus selama al-Zahir masih berkuasa di sana. Beberapa bulan kemudian, Sultan al-Zahir Baibars wafat.

Al-Jami' mengomentari peristiwa ini: perlu dicatat bahwa sultan tidak mau mengambil tindakan sebelum mendapat fatwa ulama — menunjukkan betapa besarnya penghormatan terhadap otoritas agama di masa itu. Meskipun sultan menginginkan harta itu untuk tujuan mulia, al-Nawawi menolak karena melihatnya sebagai pengambilan harta melalui cara yang tidak benar. Niat yang baik tidak membuat cara yang haram menjadi halal. Juga perlu dicatat bagaimana para ulama lainnya bereaksi: mereka tidak berdiam diri atas apa yang menimpa rekan dan pemimpin mereka, melainkan bersatu untuk membawa al-Nawawi kembali. Ini adalah sikap yang seharusnya ditunjukkan para ulama satu sama lain.

Pada kesempatan lain, penguasa mengeluarkan dekret bahwa seorang fuqaha hanya boleh mengajar di satu madrasah pada satu waktu. Ini menimbulkan kesulitan besar bagi para ulama, karena banyak madrasah yang tidak membayar gaji yang cukup untuk kehidupan mereka. Al-Nawawi — yang memahami pentingnya ulama dan pentingnya membela mereka — tidak berdiam diri, melainkan kembali menulis surat kepada penguasa untuk mengoreksi kebijakan ini. Ini menunjukkan bahwa al-Nawawi juga terlibat dalam isu-isu sosial yang tidak secara langsung berkaitan dengan nash Al-Quran dan sunnah.
content_ar:
يحكي هذا الجزء خاتمة قصة المواجهة بين الإمام النووي والسلطان الظاهر بيبرس: فبعد أن رفض النووي التوقيع على الفتوى التي تُجيز للسلطان جمع الأموال من الرعية لتمويل الجهاد — محتجاً بأنه إذا أنفق ثروته الشخصية أولاً فعند ذلك يُفتيه — أجرى السلطان عليه النفي من دمشق.

وقد قبل النووي رحمه الله النفي دون أن يُثير فتنة، مُؤثراً السلامة العامة على الصمود في وجه الطغيان. ولما غادر دمشق تحرّك العلماء ليُعيدوه، لكنه رفض وقال: «لا أدخل دمشق ما دام الظاهر فيها». وبعد أشهر قليلة مات السلطان الظاهر بيبرس، وكأن الله أجاب دعاء هذا الرجل التقي.

وقد علّق الجامي على هذه الواقعة بأنها تُبيّن كيف أن السلطان لم يكن يُقدم على أمر مهم إلا بعد استئذان العلماء، وهذا يدل على منزلة العلم والعلماء في تلك الحقبة. كما تدل على أن النية الحسنة لا تُبيح الوسيلة الحرام — وهو المبدأ الذي أصّله النووي بتثبيت موقفه.
